calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jan-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ARASH MOUSSAVIAN, Esq. "specially appearing" for plaintiff Persolve Legal Group, LLP (Tel: 818-534-3100);
No other appearances made in this matter.

PLAINTIFF PERSOLVE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The judgment debtor Jonathan Restauro's claim of exemption is granted in part.

Restauro owes Persolve Legal Group, LLP, $6,585.57. He claims he can only contribute $60 from his bi-weekly paychecks towards his debt. Restauro's financial statements indicate he earns a gross income of $5,600; a net income of $3,500 per month; and has $3430 of monthly expenses. Persolve provides an employer's return that suggests Restauro's net income is $4179.88, higher than what Restauro reported. (Comp. Restauro's Financial Statement with the Employer's Return.) Based on this document, Persolve argues it is entitled to $77.78 dollars bi-weekly, the statutory limit. (See CCP 760.050.)

The court finds Persolve's evidence is persuasive. Based on this adjusted net income and Restauro's reported monthly expenses, Restauro has not shown he can only pay $60 bi-weekly. The court orders $77.78 allocated bi-weekly towards the debt's satisfaction.

Order signed and copies mailed to parties and levying officer.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
